title: GitHub Actions - CD & Automation
summary: Extend your CI pipeline into Continuous Deployment and automation. Learn to deploy applications, manage secrets, use environments, create reusable workflows, and automate common tasks like releases and notifications.

resources.title: GitHub Actions - Deploying with GitHub Actions
GitHub Actions CI/CD Pipeline - Full Tutorial

quiz.title: Day 14 Quiz: GitHub Actions CD & Automation

questions.questionText: How do you store sensitive values like API keys in GitHub Actions?
questions.explanation: GitHub Secrets (Settings > Secrets and variables > Actions) store encrypted values that can be accessed in workflows using ${{ secrets.SECRET_NAME }}. They are never exposed in logs.

options.text: Hardcode them in the workflow YAML file
Store them in a .env file in the repository
Use GitHub Secrets and reference them with ${{ secrets.NAME }}
Pass them as workflow dispatch inputs

questions.questionText: What are GitHub Environments used for?
questions.explanation: Environments (like staging, production) allow you to set environment-specific secrets, configure protection rules (approvals, wait timers), and track deployment history.

options.text: Setting up different operating systems for runners
Configuring deployment targets with protection rules and environment-specific secrets

questions.explanation: The 'workflow_dispatch' trigger enables manual execution of a workflow from the GitHub Actions UI. You can also define input parameters that the user provides when triggering.

options.text: Manual triggering of a workflow from the GitHub UI
Triggering when a workflow in another repo completes
Triggering when a Docker image is pushed

questions.questionText: How can you reuse workflow logic across multiple repositories?
questions.explanation: Reusable workflows and composite actions let you define workflow logic once and call it from multiple workflows. Reusable workflows use 'workflow_call' trigger and are referenced with 'uses: org/repo/.github/workflows/file.yml@ref'.

options.text: Copy and paste the YAML files
Use reusable workflows or composite actions
Create a Git submodule for workflows
You cannot share workflows across repos

tasks.title: Create a CD Deployment Workflow
tasks.description:
1. Create a deployment workflow `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`:
   ```yaml
   name: Deploy
   on:
     push:
       branches: [main]
     workflow_dispatch:
   jobs:
     build:
       runs-on: ubuntu-latest
       steps:
         - uses: actions/checkout@v4
         - name: Build
           run: echo 'Building application...'
         - name: Upload artifact
           uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
           with:
             name: build-output
             path: .
     deploy-staging:
       needs: build
       runs-on: ubuntu-latest
       environment: staging
       steps:
         - name: Download artifact
           uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
           with:
             name: build-output
         - name: Deploy to staging
           run: echo 'Deploying to staging...'
     deploy-production:
       needs: deploy-staging
       runs-on: ubuntu-latest
       environment: production
       steps:
         - name: Download artifact
           uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
           with:
             name: build-output
         - name: Deploy to production
           run: echo 'Deploying to production...'
   ```
2. Push and observe the pipeline in the Actions tab
3. Set up environments in your repo settings (Settings > Environments)
4. Add a protection rule requiring approval for production
hints: Create environments via Settings > Environments > New environment
Add required reviewers as a protection rule for the production environment

tasks.title: Automate Releases with GitHub Actions
tasks.description:
1. Create a release workflow `.github/workflows/release.yml`:
   ```yaml
   name: Release
   on:
     push:
       tags:
         - 'v*'
   jobs:
     release:
       runs-on: ubuntu-latest
       permissions:
         contents: write
       steps:
         - uses: actions/checkout@v4
         - name: Create Release
           uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
           with:
             generate_release_notes: true
   ```
2. Tag a release: `git tag v1.0.0 && git push origin v1.0.0`
3. Watch the workflow create a GitHub release automatically
4. Add a scheduled workflow that runs daily:
   ```yaml
   on:
     schedule:
       - cron: '0 9 * * 1-5'  # 9 AM UTC, Mon-Fri
   ```
5. Explore the GitHub Actions Marketplace for useful actions
6. Create a matrix strategy to test across multiple OS versions:
   ```yaml
   strategy:
     matrix:
       os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest]
   runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
   ```
hints: Semantic versioning (v1.0.0) is the standard: major.minor.patch
The 'permissions' key scopes the GITHUB_TOKEN — 'contents: write' is needed to create releases
